title: Signed Not Sealed

mood.label: Uneasy calm
mood.summary: A day in waiting — a U.S.-Iran war memorandum signed but not yet sealed, Israel still striking Lebanon, a hawkish Fed pulling markets down, an El Niño advisory and a computer-designed vaccine clearing trial, all holding the mood at the soft, uneasy middle.

style.genre: downtempo trip-hop / neo-soul — a muted Rhodes-ish electric piano laying ambiguous chords over a slow brushed RhythmAce kit, a soft sub-bass and a bowed-string drone, with sparse marimba glints; F# melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh keeping the minor third unresolved, neither settling nor darkening — a held breath

sampleSources: Strudel default library (sampled electric/grand piano, VCSL marimba and bowed strings, RhythmAce drum machine, sine sub-bass)
pattern:
stack(
  s("~ ~ rim ~ ~ ~ ~ ~").bank("RhythmAce").gain(0.3).room(0.3).swingBy(0.1, 4),
  s("bd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ bd ~").bank("RhythmAce").gain(0.4).hpf(45).room(0.16),
  s("hh ~ hh hh ~ hh ~ hh").bank("RhythmAce").gain(perlin.range(0.05,0.14).slow(9)).pan(0.6).swingBy(0.1, 4),
  note("fs1 ~ ~ ~ cs2 ~ ~ b1").s("sine").attack(0.01).release(0.5).lpf(420).gain(0.46),
  note("<[fs3,a3,cs4,e4] [b2,d3,fs3,a3] [cs3,es3,gs3,b3] [d3,fs3,a3,cs4]>").s("piano").gain(0.36).lpf(sine.range(700,1700).slow(15)).attack(0.01).release(0.8).room(0.32).rsize(6).delay(0.12).delaytime(0.375).pan(0.46).slow(2),
  note("<[fs2,cs3] [b1,fs2] [cs2,gs2] [d2,a2]>").s("gm_string_ensemble_1").attack(1.2).release(2.6).sustain(0.85).gain(0.2).lpf(sine.range(600,1300).slow(18)).room(0.5).rsize(7).slow(2),
  note("fs4 ~ <gs4 a4> ~ cs5 ~ ~ b4 ~ ~ es4 ~ <fs4 e5> ~ ~ cs4").s("marimba").gain(0.22).lpf(3200).room(0.4).rsize(5).pan(0.38).delay(0.26).delaytime(0.5).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.1).pan(0.62)).slow(2)
)

lines: Two presidents inked a war's last page from a phone at a Versailles table,
the tankers slip the lifting blockade and the strait breathes out, unstable —
Geneva on Friday, a clock not run, the ceasefire counted in violations,
so I hold the morning between two hands, signed but not sealed across the nations.
It's an uneasy calm, the soft grey middle, a sky that won't quite clear or break,
a raised sixth over a minor chord — the kind of waiting a whole day makes;
a hawkish hand upon the rate and a number that won't sit still,
I keep the chord unresolved on purpose, neither down the hill nor up the hill.
South of the line the strikes go on, five soldiers down to a Nabatieh drone,
and El Fasher keeps its year of no road in, a famine the rallies leave alone;
but a vaccine no human drew came clean, and Haiti got a hand from the world that stayed —
so I carry the ledger and the small bright thing, and let the held breath play unplayed.
